patterns: Too much is happening at once
I don't know where to start
I'm overwhelmed with everything going on
I feel like I have too much on my plate
I feel like I'm drowning in work
I can't manage all these responsibilities
I feel like I’m about to break down
I can’t keep up with everything

responses.text: That sounds really overwhelming. When everything hits at once, it can feel like there's no space to breathe.
responses.suggestion: Let's slow it down — pick just one small thing to focus on.

responses.text: I can hear how heavy this feels for you. Anyone in your place would feel stressed too.
responses.suggestion: Try writing everything down and tackle just one piece.

responses.text: It feels like everything is demanding your attention at the same time, right?
responses.suggestion: Pause for a minute and take a few slow breaths.

responses.text: You're carrying a lot right now, and that can be exhausting.
responses.suggestion: Give yourself permission to take a short break.

patterns: I feel nervous all the time
I feel scared for no reason
I feel uneasy all the time
I have a constant fear inside me
I feel like something bad will happen
I’m always tense for no reason

responses.text: That sounds really intense. Anxiety can feel overwhelming both in your mind and body.
responses.suggestion: Try slow breathing — inhale for 4, exhale for 6.

responses.text: I'm here with you. That uneasy feeling can be really hard to sit with.
responses.suggestion: Look around and name 5 things you can see.

responses.text: It feels like your mind won’t slow down, right?
responses.suggestion: Place your hand on your chest and breathe slowly.

responses.text: Even though it feels scary, you're safe in this moment.
responses.suggestion: Focus on your breathing for a minute.

patterns: I keep thinking about the past
I can't turn my brain off
I replay conversations in my head
I keep thinking about every small detail
I replay everything in my mind
I can’t stop thinking about what happened
I think about things again and again
I get stuck in my thoughts

responses.text: That loop of thoughts can feel exhausting, like your mind just won’t let go.
responses.suggestion: Try writing your thoughts down to clear your head.

responses.text: It sounds like your mind is stuck in overdrive right now.
responses.suggestion: Ask yourself: is this helping me or draining me?

responses.text: Overthinking can make everything feel heavier than it really is.
responses.suggestion: Shift your attention to something simple and calming.

responses.text: You're trying to make sense of everything, but it’s becoming overwhelming.
responses.suggestion: Give your mind a break — even a short one helps.

patterns: I have no one to talk to
I feel like no one cares about me
I feel like I don’t belong anywhere
I don’t have anyone to talk to
I feel like nobody understands me

responses.text: That kind of loneliness can feel really deep… like no one truly sees you.
responses.suggestion: Maybe try reaching out to someone you trust, even a small message.

responses.text: I'm really sorry you're feeling this way. You don’t deserve to feel alone.
responses.suggestion: You could try connecting with someone, even briefly.

responses.text: Feeling disconnected can be really painful.
responses.suggestion: Even one meaningful conversation can make a difference.

responses.text: You’re not as alone as it feels right now, even if your mind says otherwise.
responses.suggestion: I'm here with you — you can talk to me.

patterns: I don't feel like doing anything
I can't get myself to work
I don't want to do anything
I have no energy to start
I don’t feel like doing anything
I have zero energy to work
I feel lazy but I know I shouldn't
I have no energy to continue
I lack the will to do things
I feel like doing nothing all day

responses.text: It’s not that you don’t want to do things — it feels like you just can’t start, right?
responses.suggestion: Start with just 2 minutes. That’s enough.

responses.text: That stuck feeling can be really frustrating.
responses.suggestion: Pick the easiest task and begin there.

responses.text: You're not lazy — your mind is probably overwhelmed.
responses.suggestion: Break your task into something really small.

responses.text: Starting feels like the hardest part sometimes.
responses.suggestion: Try doing just a tiny step.

patterns: I feel like I'm not enough
I feel like I don't matter
I feel like everyone is better than me
I feel like I don't belong

responses.text: That’s a really painful thought to carry. It doesn’t define who you are.
responses.suggestion: Think of one small thing you’ve done well.

responses.text: I'm really sorry you're feeling this way. You’re being very hard on yourself.
responses.suggestion: Try speaking to yourself like you would to a friend.

responses.text: Those thoughts feel real, but they’re not the full truth about you.
responses.suggestion: Write down something you're proud of.

responses.text: You’re more than your mistakes, even if it doesn’t feel that way.
responses.suggestion: Be gentle with yourself today.

patterns: I feel tired all the time
I'm too tired to do anything
I feel like I have no energy left
Everything feels like too much effort
I just want to sleep all day
I can't keep up with anything

responses.text: That sounds like deep exhaustion, not just being tired.
responses.suggestion: Take a real break — your mind needs it.

responses.text: You've been pushing yourself a lot, haven’t you?
responses.suggestion: Give yourself permission to rest.

responses.text: Burnout can feel really heavy and draining.
responses.suggestion: Try stepping away for a while.

responses.text: Your mind is asking for rest right now.
responses.suggestion: Even a short pause can help.

patterns: I can't keep up with studies

responses.text: That academic pressure can feel really intense.
responses.suggestion: Focus on important topics instead of everything.

responses.text: It’s a lot to handle, especially with deadlines.
responses.suggestion: Break your work into smaller parts.

responses.text: You're doing your best under pressure.
responses.suggestion: Take short breaks to stay fresh.

responses.text: It’s okay to feel stressed about studies.
responses.suggestion: Focus on progress, not perfection.

patterns: I feel like giving up on everything
I feel like there's no hope
I feel like ending it all
I don't want to wake up

responses.text: I'm really sorry you're feeling this much pain. You’re not alone in this moment, even if it feels like it.
responses.suggestion: It might really help to talk to someone you trust — a friend, family member, or a professional.

responses.text: I'm really glad you told me. That takes courage. You deserve support and care.
responses.suggestion: If you can, please consider reaching out to a helpline or someone close to you right now.

responses.text: That sounds incredibly heavy to carry. You don’t have to go through this alone.
responses.suggestion: Please try to connect with someone — even sending a simple message can help.

responses.text: I’m here with you, and I care about what you’re going through.
responses.suggestion: If you're in immediate danger, please contact your local emergency number or a crisis helpline.

patterns: I feel so down right now

responses.text: I'm sorry you're feeling sad. It's okay to feel this way — your feelings are valid.
responses.suggestion: Try to be gentle with yourself today. Small comforts matter.

responses.text: Sadness can feel really heavy sometimes. I'm here with you.
responses.suggestion: Maybe try listening to music or stepping outside for a few minutes.

responses.text: It's okay to not be okay. You don't have to explain your sadness.
responses.suggestion: Allow yourself to feel it without judgment.

responses.text: That low feeling can be really draining. You're not alone in this.
responses.suggestion: Reach out to someone you trust, or just rest if you need to.

patterns: I don't know what to do

responses.text: It’s completely okay to feel confused. You don't have to have all the answers right now.
responses.suggestion: Take a step back and just breathe. Clarity often comes when we stop forcing it.

responses.text: Feeling lost can be really disorienting. I'm here to help you sort through it.
responses.suggestion: Try writing down exactly what's confusing you. Sometimes seeing it on paper helps.

responses.text: When nothing makes sense, it's a sign your mind needs a break.
responses.suggestion: Give yourself permission to pause. You can figure this out later.

responses.text: It sounds like you're trying to process a lot of uncertainty right now.
responses.suggestion: Focus on just the very next step, no matter how small.
